overall_justification: The edition features multiple articles and commentary expressing strong opposition to the Taubaté agreement, focusing on concerns about altering the monetary standard and potential damage to Brazil's credibility with creditors.  The criticism of the agreement's core tenets, especially the proposed loan and its link to currency manipulation, aligns with core Orthodox principles.  While some factual reporting exists, the dominant framing and editorial voice clearly lean towards an Orthodox perspective.

supporting_evidence.quote: O convênio, segundo foi deliberado na conferência de hontem em Petropolis, escapa á competencia do poder executivo que, somente depois das decisões do Congresso Nacional, poderá tomar delle conhecimento.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights the newspaper's concern about the legality and due process surrounding the agreement, a common Orthodox argument.

supporting_evidence.quote: Como os leitores vêem, o sr. Rodrigues Alves recusou a sua approvação ao convênio de Taubaté, primeiro, porque julga não ter competencia para o fazer; segundo, porque s. ex. é contrario, á vista dos compromissos do Brasil com os seus credores internos e externos, á quebrado nosso padrão monetario.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This passage emphasizes President Alves' rejection, focusing on his concerns about broken commitments to creditors, a key Orthodox argument. The newspaper prominently features this rejection, signaling its own agreement with this stance.

supporting_evidence.quote: O sr. Rodrigues Alves interpretou, nesta emergencia, com perfeita fidelidade o pensamento do povo brasileiro que não está disposto a embarcar em novas e desconhecidas aventuras financeiras.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote demonstrates the newspaper's framing of the president's rejection as reflecting the will of the people, further reinforcing the Orthodox perspective of caution and stability.
